stance_taxonomy.recognition: treats PA as a diagnosable phenomenon; operates or endorses PA-specific interventions / reunification
stance_taxonomy.critique: argues PA construct is misused in family court, especially against DV survivors; advocates trauma-informed alternatives
stance_taxonomy.middle: engages both family-violence and alienating-behaviour frames; AFCC-aligned, differential-diagnosis / RRD / PCCP approach

practitioners.name: Richard A. Warshak, PhD
practitioners.practice: Independent practice (Dallas, TX); Clinical Professor of Psychology, UT Southwestern Medical Center
practitioners.specialty: Family Bridges reunification workshop; Pluto Center co-founder
programs_or_publications: Family Bridges workshop (co-developed with Randy Rand)
Warshak (2010) Family Court Review 48(1):48-80
Warshak (2019) Journal of Divorce & Remarriage 60(8) outcomes paper
Divorce Poison (HarperCollins, rev. 2010)
practitioners.hook: Author of the single most-cited Family Bridges outcomes paper; unavoidable in any recognition directory.
practitioners.notes: Specific Texas license number not publicly confirmed on warshak.com.

practitioners.name: Linda Gottlieb, LMFT, LCSW-R
practitioners.practice: Turning Points for Families (TPFF), New York City
practitioners.specialty: TPFF intensive in-home reunification (Minuchin structural family therapy lineage)
practitioners.stance_source: https://lindagottlieb.com/ — practice statement: 'parental alienation is a form of psychological child abuse with lifetime consequences'
programs_or_publications: Turning Points for Families intensive
The Parental Alienation Syndrome (Charles C. Thomas, 2012)
Practice claim: testified in 1,500+ adversarial custody disputes
practitioners.hook: TPFF is one of the programs critiqued in Geffner et al. (2022); naming her engages a central reunification operator.
practitioners.notes: Endorsed historically by Bernet and Salvador Minuchin.

practitioners.name: Craig Childress, PsyD
practitioners.practice: Independent clinical psychology practice; AB-PA model author
practitioners.specialty: Attachment-Based Model of Parental Alienation (AB-PA); DSM-5 attachment/delusional pathway framing
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.amazon.com/dp/0996114505 — Childress (2015) An Attachment-Based Model of Parental Alienation: Foundations
programs_or_publications: AB-PA Foundations (Oaksong Press, 2015)
Catalogue of Concerns critique of AFCC evaluator training
Owl Project AI-augmented forensic-evaluator vitae review
practitioners.hook: Recognition camp's internal dissident — DSM-5-anchored framing without PASG five-factor; useful for parents seeking diagnostic rigour outside PASG.
practitioners.notes: Washington psychology license #61538481 per practice/blog; openly critical of both AFCC training and PASG five-factor.

practitioners.name: Amy J.L. Baker, PhD
practitioners.practice: Director of Research, Vincent J. Fontana Center for Child Protection (New York Foundling)
practitioners.specialty: Adult survivors of PA; 17 alienating-strategies taxonomy
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.amazon.com/dp/0393705196 — Adult Children of Parental Alienation Syndrome (W.W. Norton, 2007)
programs_or_publications: Adult Children of Parental Alienation Syndrome (Norton, 2007)
Baker & Darnall (2006) 17 alienating strategies, Journal of Divorce & Remarriage 45:97-124
Co-parenting with a Toxic Ex (2014)
practitioners.hook: 17 strategies is the most-replicated PA measurement instrument in peer review — empirical entry point to recognition-camp claims.
practitioners.notes: Research psychologist (PhD Developmental, Teachers College Columbia 1989); not clinically licensed — consultation only.

practitioners.name: William Bernet, MD
practitioners.practice: Professor Emeritus of Psychiatry, Vanderbilt University School of Medicine; founder/first president, Parental Alienation Study Group (PASG)
practitioners.specialty: Forensic psychiatric evaluation in PA; advocate for DSM/ICD recognition
programs_or_publications: PASG (founded 2013; ~240 members across 40 countries)
Five-Factor Model of PA diagnosis (JAACAP, 2022)
Parental Alienation, DSM-5, and ICD-11 (Charles C. Thomas, 2010)
Parental Alienation: Science and Law (2020, with Lorandos)
practitioners.hook: Global organising figure for recognition camp; opens the entire PASG network.
practitioners.notes: Per Vanderbilt profile: testified ~300 times in 24 states. Post-emeritus TN license status not publicly confirmed.

practitioners.name: J. Michael Bone, PhD
practitioners.practice: Independent PA consultation practice (Winter Park, FL); co-founder NAOPAS
practitioners.specialty: PA expert witness; consultation, evaluation, court testimony (US, Spain, Italy)
programs_or_publications: Bone & Walsh Four Criteria framework (Florida Bar Journal, 1999)
NAOPAS co-founder (with Robert Evans, 2017)
Worked directly with Richard Gardner on multiple cases (per practice bio)
practitioners.primary_contact: 201 W Canton Ave, Suite 225, Winter Park, FL 32789; (407) 645-0662; https://www.jmichaelbone.com/contact.html
practitioners.hook: Bone-Walsh Four Criteria is still the most-cited Florida-bar PA detection reference; FL attorneys know this paper.
practitioners.notes: Closed clinical practice 2006; consultant-only since. Current Florida licensure status not publicly confirmed.

practitioners.name: Robert A. Evans, PhD
practitioners.practice: The Center For Human Potential of America, Inc. (Clearwater, FL); co-founder NAOPAS
practitioners.specialty: Forensic psychology / PA expert testimony; Family Bridges-trained reunification specialist
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.amazon.com/Essentials-Parental-Alienation-Syndrome-Childrens/dp/1937698009 — Evans & Bone (2011) The Essentials of Parental Alienation Syndrome
programs_or_publications: The Essentials of Parental Alienation Syndrome (Unhooking Books, 2011)
NAOPAS co-founder
Trained in Warshak/Rand Family Bridges as reunification specialist
practitioners.hook: High-volume expert witness — 38+ courts in 36 states per JurisPro; useful for press volume signalling.
practitioners.notes: Florida Licensed School Psychologist (since 1987) — flag carefully; not full-scope psychologist license.

practitioners.name: Susan Heitler, PhD
practitioners.practice: Independent clinical psychology / consultation (Denver, CO); formerly Rose Medical Center 1975-2020
practitioners.specialty: Couples therapy + parental alienation; popular-press PA writing
programs_or_publications: The Power of Two (couples-skills workbook, New Harbinger)
Psychology Today PA series (>1M aggregate views per bio)
APA Fellow (2013)
practitioners.hook: Psychology Today readership is a recognition-camp megaphone; unlocks popular-press placements.
practitioners.notes: Recognition-leaning but framed as 'conflict resolution' rather than PASG-core.

practitioners.name: Joyanna Silberg, PhD
practitioners.practice: President, Leadership Council on Child Abuse & Interpersonal Violence; independent forensic psychotherapy (Baltimore, MD); formerly Sheppard Pratt until 2019
practitioners.specialty: Child dissociation/trauma; forensic assessment of child sexual abuse; critique of PA misuse in custody
practitioners.stance_source: https://leadershipcouncil.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/Silberg-and-Dallam-2019-Abusers-gaining-custody-in-family-courts.pdf — Silberg & Dallam (2019) Journal of Child Custody 16(2)
programs_or_publications: Silberg & Dallam (2019) case series of 27 overturned custody decisions
The Child Survivor: Healing Developmental Trauma and Dissociation (Routledge)
Past-president, International Society for the Study of Trauma & Dissociation
Mercer & Silberg (eds., 2024) Challenging Parental Alienation (Routledge)
practitioners.hook: Silberg & Dallam (2019) anchors the entire critique-camp evidence base; entry to the DV-trauma-informed network.
practitioners.notes: Maryland psychology license number not publicly confirmed on site. Routinely opposed by PASG-affiliated experts.

practitioners.name: Stephanie Dallam, PhD, RN
practitioners.practice: Researcher, Leadership Council on Child Abuse & Interpersonal Violence
practitioners.specialty: Research critique of PAS history (Gardner provenance) and reunification programs
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/15379418.2016.1219974 — Dallam & Silberg (2016) Journal of Child Custody 13(2-3):134-143
programs_or_publications: Dallam & Silberg (2016) foreseeable-harm critique of reunification
Dallam (2001) 'The Parental Alienation Syndrome: Is It Scientific?' in St. Charles & Crook (eds.)
Silberg & Dallam (2019) overturned-decisions case series
practitioners.hook: Foundational scientific-validity critique of PAS — invaluable for press when recognition camp claims 'settled science.'
practitioners.notes: Researcher (PhD Social Welfare, University of Kansas); RN is the licensable health credential. Not a practising therapist.

practitioners.name: Robert Geffner, PhD, ABPP, ABN
practitioners.practice: Founding President, Institute on Violence, Abuse & Trauma (IVAT), San Diego; Distinguished Research Professor (Retired), Alliant International University
practitioners.specialty: Family violence, IPV, trauma-informed forensic critique of reunification programs
practitioners.stance_source: https://doi.org/10.1080/26904586.2022.2049462 — Mercer & Drew (2022) and Geffner et al. (2022) special issue, Journal of Family Trauma, Child Custody & Child Development 19(3-4)
programs_or_publications: 2022 JFTCCCD special issue critiquing TPFF, Family Bridges, OBFC
IVAT (founded as FVSAI, 1984)
Editor of multiple peer-reviewed family-trauma journals
practitioners.hook: Editor of the 2022 JFTCCCD special issue — direct line to the most-current academic critique of reunification camps.
practitioners.notes: TX Licensed Psychologist + CA LMFT per IVAT bio. Dual licensure gives cross-jurisdiction expert-witness reach.

practitioners.name: Jennifer J. Harman, PhD
practitioners.practice: Associate Professor, Department of Psychology, Colorado State University
practitioners.specialty: Empirical / prevalence research on PA; power dynamics in intimate relationships
programs_or_publications: Harman, Kruk & Hines (2018) Psychological Bulletin prevalence/conceptual paper
Harman & Matthewson (2020) Parents Acting Badly (Familization Press)
President, International Council on Shared Parenting; PASG Board
practitioners.hook: Harman 2018 is the most-cited prevalence claim recognition camp uses — and corrects the source brief 'Hardman' typo.
practitioners.notes: Original brief listed 'Jennifer Hardman, LMFT' under critique; no such person publicly verifiable. Closest verifiable name is Harman, who is firmly recognition-camp. Academic researcher, not clinically licensed.

practitioners.name: Joan B. Kelly, PhD
practitioners.practice: Independent practice (Corte Madera, CA); retired-status practitioner; AFCC fellow
practitioners.specialty: Architect of the 'alienated child' reformulation; RRD framing
programs_or_publications: Kelly & Johnston (2001) reformulation paper
Surviving the Breakup (with Wallerstein, 1980)
practitioners.primary_contact: not publicly confirmed; route via AFCC https://www.afccnet.org/
practitioners.hook: Kelly-Johnston 2001 is the single most-cited middle-ground PA paper; every AFCC-aligned evaluator knows it.
practitioners.notes: Active practice contact is the principal gap. Current CA licensure status not publicly confirmed.

practitioners.name: Janet R. Johnston, PhD
practitioners.practice: Professor Emeritus, Department of Justice Studies, San Jose State University
practitioners.specialty: Longitudinal counselling research with high-conflict litigating couples; alienated vs. estranged children
programs_or_publications: Kelly & Johnston (2001) reformulation
In the Name of the Child (2nd ed., Springer, 2009)
Impasses of Divorce (1988)
Johnston & Sullivan (2020) common-ground synthesis
practitioners.hook: Johnston-Sullivan (2020) is the most-recent middle-camp synthesis; bridge to OBFC network.
practitioners.notes: Post-emeritus clinical licensure status not publicly confirmed. Primarily academic.

practitioners.name: Leslie M. Drozd, PhD
practitioners.practice: Independent forensic psychology practice (Newport Beach, CA); AFCC Board of Directors; OBFC Board
practitioners.specialty: Custody evaluation; differential analysis of family violence, alienation, gatekeeping; decision-tree methodology
practitioners.stance_source: https://global.oup.com/academic/product/parenting-plan-evaluations-9780199396580 — Drozd, Saini & Olesen (eds., 2016) Parenting Plan Evaluations 2nd ed., Oxford
programs_or_publications: Co-edited AFCC Model Standards for Child Custody Evaluations (2006)
AFCC Guidelines for Examining Intimate Partner Violence (2016)
Parenting Plan & Child Custody Evaluations: Using Decision Trees (PRP, 2013)
2013 AFCC John E. Van Duzer Distinguished Service Award
practitioners.hook: Decision-tree framework is the AFCC-canonical evaluator methodology; direct entry to the middle-camp evaluator network.
practitioners.notes: Among the few practitioners trusted by both camps for testimony — high credibility signal. CA Licensed Psychologist + LMFT.

practitioners.name: Nancy W. Olesen, PhD
practitioners.specialty: Court-appointed custody and dependency evaluations; mandatory training for California evaluators
practitioners.stance_source: Co-editor with Drozd & Saini of Parenting Plan Evaluations (Oxford, 2016) and Parenting Plan & Child Custody Evaluations (PRP, 2013); contributor to AFCC Model Standards
programs_or_publications: Parenting Plan Evaluations (Oxford, 2016)
Parenting Plan & Child Custody Evaluations (PRP, 2013)
AFCC Model Standards contributor
practitioners.primary_contact: not publicly confirmed on a personal site; route via AFCC California chapter
practitioners.hook: Co-author of the AFCC evaluator-training canon; useful as referent but contact is the gap.
practitioners.notes: No standalone practice website surfaced. Practice contact is the principal gap.

practitioners.name: Marsha Kline Pruett, PhD, MSL, ABPP
practitioners.practice: Maconda Brown O'Connor Professor, Smith College School for Social Work; independent practice and consulting
practitioners.specialty: Parental gatekeeping; parent-child contact problems (PCCP); differential frameworks; survey research on PA belief systems
practitioners.stance_source: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/fcre.12716 — Kline Pruett et al. (2023) Family Court Review 61(2):372-394
programs_or_publications: Kline Pruett (2023) FCR survey of belief systems
Sullivan, Kline Pruett et al. (2024) PCCP synthesis, Family Court Review 62(2)
Bench Book for Assessing Parental Gatekeeping in Parenting Disputes (Smith College Scholar Works, 2018)
practitioners.hook: Her 2023 FCR survey is the only empirical study of how professionals themselves use the PA construct; uniquely positions her as a 'data-on-the-debate' interview source.
practitioners.notes: ABPP Board Certified. Among the few middle voices whose work measures the field's own polarisation.

practitioners.name: Robin M. Deutsch, PhD, ABPP
practitioners.practice: Director, Center of Excellence for Children, Families and the Law, William James College (Newton, MA); co-founder OBFC
practitioners.specialty: Overcoming Barriers Family Camp (5-day intensive); attachment-informed PCCP intervention
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.amazon.com/dp/0190235209 — Deutsch, Sullivan et al. (eds., 2016) Overcoming Parent-Child Contact Problems: Family-Based Interventions, Oxford
programs_or_publications: Co-founder, Overcoming Barriers Family Camp (with Matthew Sullivan)
Overcoming Parent-Child Contact Problems (Oxford, 2016)
20+ years Director of Forensic Services & Training, Children and the Law Program, Massachusetts General Hospital
practitioners.hook: OBFC is the most-frequently court-ordered 'middle' intervention; if you want to reach the practitioners running these camps, Deutsch is the door.
practitioners.notes: OBFC sits in the 2022 Geffner et al. critique alongside Family Bridges and TPFF — sourcing her engages that dispute.

practitioners.name: Matthew J. Sullivan, PhD
practitioners.practice: Independent forensic/clinical psychology practice (Palo Alto, CA); co-founder OBFC; past President, AFCC International (2019-20)
practitioners.specialty: OBFC family camp; parent coordination; PCCP synthesis
programs_or_publications: OBFC co-founder
Sullivan, Ward & Deutsch (2010) FCR 48(1):116-135 OBFC outcomes paper
Johnston & Sullivan (2020) common-ground synthesis
2012 AFCC California Joseph Drown Award; 2019-20 AFCC International President
practitioners.primary_contact: https://sullydoc.com/contact/ ; 411 Kipling St, Palo Alto, CA
practitioners.hook: Past AFCC International President — the institutional middle-camp voice; engages both camps on equal footing.
practitioners.notes: OBFC operationally critiqued by trauma camp (Geffner et al. 2022); same caveat as Deutsch.

excluded.name: Randy Rand, EdD
excluded.reason: California psychology license inactive since 2009 following Board of Psychology disciplinary action (gross negligence, unprofessional conduct, dishonesty); Family Bridges/Family Workshop operates as 'educational workshop' outside CA Board of Psychology oversight.

excluded.name: Steven Pumphrey, PhD
excluded.reason: Could not verify from public sources — no practice site, no court records, no peer-reviewed PA publication surfaced.

excluded.name: Jennifer Hardman, LMFT
excluded.reason: No such person publicly verifiable in PA literature; closest verifiable name is Jennifer J. Harman, PhD (Colorado State, PASG board, recognition camp). Included as Harman correction entry.

excluded.name: William G. Austin, PhD
excluded.reason: Verified (Lakewood, CO; child-custody-services.com) but stance is gatekeeping-research / methodologically middle without strong public PA-specific positioning. Omitted as borderline; eligible for v1.1.

excluded.name: Demosthenes Lorandos, PhD, JD
excluded.reason: Retired from active practice; covered in US-lawyers batch.

excluded.name: Karen Woodall, Nick Woodall, Sietske Dijkstra, Barbara Jo Fidler
excluded.reason: Non-US (UK, NL, Canada respectively); reserved for those country batches.
